title: Review, prove, and land grapheme interaction
description:
## Goal
Turn the implementation into reviewable, durable evidence and land it without overclaiming.

## Non-goals
Do not close the campaign on narrow tests, green formatting alone, or example-only behavior.

## Required Skills
lynx,rook,badger,wren
design: Run adversarial correctness/API and real-vs-mirage reviews, write the proof record and migration handoff, inspect the native path, run every local/MSRV/no_std/wasm/rustdoc gate, obtain green PR and merge-group matrices, then close the child and parent Beads with exact evidence.
acceptance_criteria: No Must findings remain; Design-0011 and public rustdoc match implementation; proof covers every matrix row; no dependency, std, unsafe, or ownership drift occurs; clean coherent commits land through protected CI; Beads/design/proof handoff is complete.

title: Drive grapheme interaction through the native editor
description:
## Goal
Prove the completed unit and transaction model through the native showcase, Winit event feed, and host-query composition surface.

## Non-goals
Do not move Backspace policy, pointer gestures, IME protocol mechanics, or presentation into core.

## Required Skills
otter,marten,badger,wren
design: Exercise pointer hits and drags, left/right movement, Backspace/Delete, multiple carets, snapshot/generated composition units, commit/cancel, mixed bidi, Arabic marks, and paragraph-local retained work through public APIs only.
acceptance_criteria: The release showcase edits the full Design-0011 corpus without panic, partial deletion, duplicate geometry, or lost semantic action routing; IME preedit publishes no revision, commit publishes once, cancel reuses work, and unrelated paragraphs retain formation.
notes:
Core, adapter, scene, and transaction prerequisites are complete in 56ce91e and 7349677. Native proof will now make the existing decomposed cafe specimen cross real semantic leaves without changing its visible text, then exercise pointer placement, Backspace, multi-caret, IME, bidi, semantic action routing, and retained work through the release showcase path.
Native release proof is complete. The visible decomposed cafe specimen now spans three real semantic leaves while preserving identical styling and appearance. Showcase tests exercise exact pointer endpoints, left/right movement, Backspace and Delete across the full multi-leaf grapheme, mixed-bidi visual drag, two-caret atomic insertion, semantic activation versus drag, transient IME preedit, cancel with all ten committed paragraphs reused, commit with at least nine sibling paragraphs reused, Chinese system fallback, and Arabic mark rendering. cargo test and clippy for underwood_showcase are green; cargo run --release was visually inspected.
Adversarial matrix review found one missing conjunction: native multi-caret insertion and native full-grapheme deletion were separately proven, but multi-caret full-unit deletion was not. Added a release-showcase regression with two pointer-derived carets sharing one semantic leaf; Backspace removes the split grapheme and a second unit in one publication.
close_reason: The native showcase now product-proves source-complete grapheme interaction through public APIs.

title: Publish same-paragraph multi-leaf range transactions
description:
## Goal
Let one validated selection replace ordered ranges across semantic leaves of one paragraph without performing structural edits.

## Non-goals
Do not remove, merge, reorder, or restyle leaves; do not join paragraphs; do not introduce durable anchors.

## Required Skills
alder,marten,cedar
design: Validate all ranges and independent-selection conflicts before staging. Preserve leaf identity and role, apply leaf-local operations in reverse document order, insert once at the first logical source, publish once, and return the new-revision caret in the insertion leaf. Reject cross-paragraph selections explicitly.
acceptance_criteria: Split-leaf EGC deletion empties both sources atomically; cross-leaf replacement inserts once and preserves structure; multi-caret ordering is deterministic; overlap, stale, foreign, UTF-8, oversized, and cross-paragraph cases fail before staging; exactly the owning paragraph invalidates; migration and rustdoc are complete.
notes: Same-paragraph multi-leaf replacement is implemented and separately committed in 56ce91e. Validation enforces canonical original-revision ranges, global conflicts, checked per-leaf sizes, reverse application, one insertion, one publication, deterministic caret rebasing, preserved leaf structure, and explicit cross-paragraph rejection. Shared-leaf multicaret and split-EGC product tests are green.
close_reason: Atomic same-paragraph multi-leaf range transactions are complete.

title: Project source-complete units through text scenes
description:
## Goal
Project each paragraph interaction unit into all committed or generated source segments, exact endpoint owners, semantic hit slices, and one geometry record.

## Non-goals
Do not create broad semantic action bounds, fabricate geometry for zero-advance slices, or change renderer paint extent.

## Required Skills
alder,otter,marten,cedar
design: Add the committed source-complete hit unit, retain ProjectedTextRange for transient general provenance, resolve semantic identity per visual slice, and make hit/caret/selection/range geometry operate once per unit. Internal semantic-leaf boundaries are not caret stops.
acceptance_criteria: Committed and composition hits expose complete ordered sources; split semantic and snapshot/generated units preserve provenance; exact action routing still uses the hit slice; selection geometry is nonduplicated; stale and foreign positions fail; public hit migration and rustdoc are complete.
notes: Added SnapshotTextUnit and projected each prepared unit into ordered leaf-local or snapshot/generated sources. Scene geometry is one record per unit; hit routing chooses the exact nonzero visual slice SemanticId; zero-advance sources remain in the unit without fabricated pointer interiors. Split semantic-leaf and composition-generated combining-mark tests prove complete sources, two endpoints, and nonduplicated geometry.
close_reason: Committed and transient scenes now preserve source-complete interaction units and exact semantic hit slices.

title: Lower Parley-analysis grapheme interaction units
description:
## Goal
Replace paragraph-adapter shaping-record interaction clusters with analysis-derived extended-grapheme units while retaining every shaped visual slice.

## Non-goals
Do not alter glyph paint ownership, add Unicode data, reconstruct bidi in Underwood, or merge OpenType ligature components.

## Required Skills
alder,otter,marten,cedar
design: Consume Analysis::is_grapheme_start once. Validate complete ordered unit coverage, slice containment, visual adjacency, line containment, checked advance sums, two endpoint sides, and cursor steps whose crossed source is the complete unit.
acceptance_criteria: Prepared output exposes source-complete units and slices; logical/visual cursor maps have no internal EGC caret; CRLF and controls remain correct; ffi components remain independently reachable; invalid backend coverage fails deterministically; public adapter migration and rustdoc are complete.
notes: Implemented the approved adapter migration: PreparedInteractionUnit owns analysis-derived EGC source, checked visual slices, aggregate advance, bidi level, boundary/whitespace, and two distinct endpoints. underwood_parley caches Analysis::is_grapheme_start ranges, groups formed shaping records without a second segmenter, rejects partial-line/scattered/mixed-level units, preserves ligature components, and drives cursor steps across whole units. CRLF and Arabic-mark public deletion proofs are green.
close_reason: Parley analysis now owns complete grapheme interaction units and cursor transitions.

title: Lock the extended-grapheme trap corpus
description:
## Goal
Make every Design-0011 correctness claim executable through the real Parley-backed public path before behavior changes are considered complete.

## Non-goals
Do not add a detached Unicode segmenter oracle, commit permanently ignored tests, or accept prepare-only evidence as editing correctness.

## Required Skills
marten,otter
design: Restore the split-leaf e + U+0301 backspace trap first, then cover precomposed/decomposed text, CRLF, emoji ZWJ, regional indicators, spacing marks, Arabic marks, ligature components, mixed bidi, multi-caret, and generated composition source. Assertions cover positions, source lists, selections, edits, and retained work.
acceptance_criteria: The corpus fails for the known shaping-record behavior before the migration and passes only when complete extended graphemes move/select/delete atomically; ffi components remain separately reachable; no test substitutes a local segmentation model for Parley analysis.
notes: Permanent corpus is now green through the real Parley-backed public path: analysis-derived cases lock decomposed/precomposed text, CRLF, emoji ZWJ, regional indicators, and spacing marks; the split-leaf e + U+0301 trap asserts complete hit sources, exact semantic routing, one PreviousLogical selection, and one atomic multi-leaf publication. Existing product tests retain Arabic marks, ffi ligature reachability, mixed bidi, multi-caret, generated composition provenance, and retained-work evidence.
close_reason: The failing behavior is replaced by permanent end-to-end source-complete grapheme proofs.

title: Approve Design-0011 grapheme interaction boundary
description:
## Decision
Approve the ownership and public migration required to replace shaping-record deletion with source-complete extended-grapheme interaction units.

## Rationale
Shaping records are not Unicode extended grapheme clusters. The split-leaf repro deletes only a combining mark today, while a second segmenter in Underwood would duplicate Parley and risk Unicode-version disagreement.

## Alternatives Considered
1. Keep shaping records as deletion units: rejected as observably incorrect.
2. Segment again in Underwood: rejected as duplicate Unicode physics and dependency pressure.
3. Consume Parley analysis boundaries and project sources: recommended because it preserves crate ownership.

## Goals
Authorize Parley-analysis-derived units with visual slices, committed/projected source-complete hits, and non-structural same-paragraph multi-leaf replacement.

## Non-goals
Do not approve a second Unicode segmenter, semantic-leaf merging, cross-paragraph structural editing, paint ownership changes, or showcase gesture policy in core.

## Required Skills
alder,shepherd
design: Design-0011 is the authority. This decision must close before production API or behavior changes begin.
acceptance_criteria: Bruce explicitly approves all three Human gate decisions in Design-0011; the approval is recorded in the design, Beads, and PR #19 before dependent implementation starts.
close_reason: Bruce Mitchener explicitly approved all three Design-0011 decisions on 2026-07-23: Parley-analysis interaction units with visual slices, source-complete committed/projected hits, and non-structural same-paragraph multi-leaf replacement.

title: Represent extended graphemes as multi-source interaction units
description:
Make logical movement and deletion operate on Unicode extended grapheme clusters even when Parley emits several shaping records or one grapheme crosses authored semantic text leaves. Preserve every leaf-local source and endpoint semantic identity instead of collapsing the unit to one range.

## Required Skills
alder,otter,marten
design: Add a source-complete multi-range interaction unit at the paragraph/scene seam. It must carry every leaf-local range, distinct endpoint ownership, visual geometry, and cursor-step deletion source without changing glyph paint ownership. Consume Parley analysis grapheme boundaries once; do not add a competing Unicode segmenter or approximate leaf boundaries.
acceptance_criteria: Precomposed and decomposed graphemes delete atomically; a base and combining mark split across two semantic leaves prepare, hit, move, select, and delete without panic or lost semantic identity; ligature components remain independently reachable; mixed bidi and IME composition variants pass; public API migration is documented; full local and remote gates pass.
notes:
Design-0011 records the human gate. Repro: a temporary focused test prepared TEXT("e") + EMPHASIS(U+0301), moved PreviousLogical from the final caret, and replaced with empty text; the desired atomic assertion failed because the base leaf remained "e" while only the mark leaf emptied. Chosen seam: consume Parley Analysis::is_grapheme_start once in underwood_parley, lower source-complete interaction units with visual slices, project them to all leaf-local sources and exact endpoint owners, and allow non-structural same-paragraph multi-leaf replacement. No production API or behavior has changed pending approval.\nDraft PR #19 carries the documentation-only human gate. Production implementation remains paused pending explicit approval of the three decisions in Design-0011.
2026-07-23: Bruce explicitly approved Design-0011. The adapter/hit public migration and same-paragraph multi-leaf range transaction are authorized; the no-segmenter, no-new-dependency, no-unsafe, no-structural-edit fences remain.

title: Specify feed and host-driven IME adapters
description:
## Decision

Underwood will expose one revisioned editable-surface and composition state machine that supports two adapter families: reduced-information event feeds and richer host-driven text protocols. The focused surface owns explicit semantic flattening and snapshot-consistent text, selection, composition, range conversion, geometry, and hit queries. Native protocol mechanics stay outside Underwood.

## Rationale

Winit and IMM32 can emit composition snapshots without querying the editor, but AppKit, UIKit, Android InputConnection, Windows TSF, and related protocols synchronously ask the host for editor state. Exact scene geometry and validated snapshot transactions are the common substrate. Treating Winit as the core model would force later platform support to bypass or distort the editor contract.

## Alternatives Considered

1. Define the core around Winit events. Rejected because it cannot answer surrounding-text, range, geometry, or point queries and does not preserve explicit replacement semantics.
2. Put ui-events traits directly in a production Underwood crate now. Rejected because the upstream reverse-query API is experimental and a dependency is unnecessary to prove the boundary.
3. Build a separate editor model per platform. Rejected because composition, selection, transaction, and retained-work invariants would diverge.

## Required Skills

alder,otter,marten,wren
design: Underwood owns a revisioned editable-surface projection and composition state machine. Platform adapters own offset encodings, synchronous protocol mechanics, coordinate transforms, locks or callbacks, and lifecycle notification. The simple feed is an adapter with less information, not the defining model.
acceptance_criteria: Design-0009 contains a protocol capability matrix and invariants for snapshot consistency, editable-surface flattening, UTF-8/UTF-16 conversion, replacement ownership, synchronous geometry and text queries, selection changes, cancel and commit, and host notifications; deterministic traces prove both adapter families can drive one core state machine without adding a production dependency.
notes:
Decision executed in Design-0009 and the IME compatibility proof. One revisioned EditableSurface serves feed-driven and host-driven protocols: it binds complete multi-selection state, text, provenance, geometry, revision, and composition epoch; converts UTF-8/UTF-16/scalar ranges; answers synchronous text/geometry/hit queries; and maps explicit host replacement ranges back into scene-validated selections. Native marked-text singularity normalizes only at composition entry and reports the selection change. No production dependency added.
The accepted protocol decision shipped with PR #14 after all eight CI jobs passed in Actions run 29934952887.
close_reason: Accepted feed-versus-host contract is fully specified and executable: both protocol traces share one revisioned state, explicit replacement ownership, complete multi-selection preservation outside singular marked text, synchronous queries, conversion, mutation mapping, and host responsibility fences.

title: Add semantic activation and land the interactive proof
description:
Finish the interactive proof with semantic hover, pressed state, pointer affordance, and activation routed from exact scene hits. Associate showcase-owned semantic node IDs with actions such as URLs while leaving navigation and permanent link schema outside core.

## Required Skills
alder,otter,marten,lynx,rook,badger,wren
design: Underwood returns an exact revision-bound position and SemanticId. The application resolves that identity through an action registry, and the host performs activation. Wrapped and mixed-bidi link-shaped content exercises the same cluster map as editing.
acceptance_criteria: Hover, press, cancellation, and activation are exact across wrapping, bidi, and glyph interiors; non-actionable content never activates; core opens no URLs and invents no permanent link schema; adversarial review finds no semantic mirage; full local and remote gates pass; design, Beads, proof artifacts, and handoff are complete.
notes:
Alder fence: the showcase owns action association and host activation; Underwood owns only exact revision-bound semantic identity and explicitly does not own URLs, navigation, or a permanent link schema. Chosen first slice: reuse existing TextHit::semantic_id and showcase semantic leaf identities; add an example-owned action registry plus hover/press/release state machine; prove wrapped mixed-bidi activation and cancellation without changing the foundational public API.
Implementation checkpoint: the showcase now binds its mixed English/Arabic source leaf to a scene-revision SemanticId registry, resolves hover/press/release exclusively through exact TextScene::hit_test, changes only leaf paint for action state, requests the native link pointer, consumes plain action presses before editor policy, cancels mismatched releases, and hands a URL-shaped value to a host-owned visible receipt without launching a browser. Focused 24-test suite and release-mode 1100x800 native inspection pass; full repository gates and adversarial review remain.
Regression found during native review: a drag beginning on the mixed-script action leaf was held as an action, and the wrapped bidi caret path could resolve only in the reciprocal direction. Fixed with a 4px click/drag transfer in showcase policy plus direction-independent visual selection ranges/order in core. Added dragging_from_action_text_selects_instead_of_activating and visual_selection_uses_the_reciprocal_caret_path. Full local gates green: fmt, Taplo, headers, typos, metadata/xtask, workspace clippy -D warnings, workspace tests, rustdoc -D warnings, Rust 1.92 MSRV, x86_64-unknown-none, and wasm32-unknown-unknown.
Adversarial review complete: fixed the premature 'host activated' status overclaim and the action-originated bidi drag failure; real-vs-mirage audit confirms host receipt is intentionally not browser navigation and action policy remains outside production crates. GitHub Actions run 29981259791 passed all eight jobs on PR #17.
Final remote evidence: PR #17 head run 29981440158 and merge-group run 29981766554 passed all eight jobs; landed as squash commit 7f61221 at 2026-07-23T05:20:35Z.
close_reason: Landed in PR #17 at 7f61221 after green head and merge-group matrices; exact semantic activation, cancellation, host receipt, action-originated mixed-bidi selection, and reciprocal visual-range regressions are complete.

title: Make the live showcase directly editable
description:
Translate native pointer, keyboard, focus, and IME events into the snapshot interaction and transaction APIs. Demonstrate click, drag, caret movement, selection, insertion, deletion, and preedit in the retained semantic document.

## Required Skills
otter,marten,badger,wren
design: The Winit showcase owns gestures, focus, blink timing, key bindings, platform IME translation, and presentation. Underwood remains host-independent and receives only explicit semantic operations.
acceptance_criteria: The release-mode showcase supports accurate click and drag through wrapped mixed-bidi and ligature text, a mixed-bidi visual selection with disjoint logical ranges, creation and rendering of multiple independent selections/carets, keyboard multi-caret editing and navigation, native preedit update/cancel/commit on the primary selection, resize reflow, and useful prepare/render timing.
notes:
Native editor is locally executable with pointer/keyboard/multi-caret/visual-bidi selection, Winit preedit/commit, fixed platform font snapshot, native Han IME regression, split prepare/render timing, and explicit viewport overflow. Renderer-boundary prerequisite und-oh0.2.9 is implemented locally and now an explicit dependency. Awaiting remote CI before closure.
Renderer prerequisite und-oh0.2.9 landed independently as PR #15 (main commit 1afd987). The rebased editor-only branch passes formatting, Clippy with denied warnings, all-feature workspace tests, denied-warning rustdoc, Rust 1.92 MSRV, repository policy, headers, spelling, metadata, x86_64-unknown-none, and wasm32-unknown-unknown. Remote editor CI remains the closure gate.
Remote Definition of Done passed for editor-only PR #16 at commit 2b13db9 in GitHub Actions run 29978412963. All eight required jobs are green across Linux, macOS, Windows, formatting and text policy, repository policy, Rust 1.92 MSRV, denied-warning rustdoc, x86_64-unknown-none, and wasm32-unknown-unknown.
close_reason: Delivered the directly editable native showcase with exact pointer and keyboard interaction, independent multi-selection, visual bidi ranges, atomic edits, Winit IME preedit and commit, system-font Han fallback, scene-derived editor overlays, split timing, migration and proof records, and all local and remote gates green in PR #16.

title: Project and retain IME composition epochs
description: Represent native preedit as an explicit generated projection over a committed replacement range. Keep committed and transient paragraph formations distinct so update, cancel, and commit obey the IME lifecycle without corrupting authored provenance. Support both one-way composition feeds and host-driven protocols that synchronously query and mutate an editable text surface.
design: A composition epoch carries its identity, committed base revision, replacement range, generated preedit, selected preedit range, and clauses. A revisioned editable-surface projection maps semantic snapshot positions to platform offsets and answers selection, surrounding-text, geometry, and hit-test queries. Winit-style feeds and AppKit, UIKit, Android, or TSF-style host conversations adapt to this same state machine.
acceptance_criteria: Preedit updates render and select generated text without mutating the committed snapshot; cancel restores committed geometry without reshaping it; commit publishes once; stale epochs and invalid ranges fail explicitly; Arabic and combining preedit cases are covered; both a simple event feed and synchronous host-query trace pass against the same composition state; full gates pass.
notes:
The ui-events audit used current windows-hardening plus the apple-text-ime branch. ui-events carries transport events; its ui-text-input experiment exposes the reverse host-query direction. Underwood must make the simple Winit feed and richer host-driven protocol adapters over one revisioned composition/editable-surface model.
Implemented checked CompositionSession epochs, explicit generated provenance, projected CompositionScene geometry/hits/movement, separate committed/transient caches, revisioned EditableSurface bindings, host replacement selection mapping, and real Parley Arabic/combining proofs. Event-feed and synchronous host traces pass; cancel publishes nothing and reuses all committed work; commit publishes once and retains unaffected paragraphs. Full workspace, rustdoc, MSRV, bare-metal, Wasm, policy, text, and Beads gates pass locally. Evidence: docs/proof/ime-composition-review-2026-07-22.md.
Remote proof: PR #14 (https://github.com/forest-rs/underwood/pull/14) passed all eight required CI jobs in Actions run 29934952887 and landed on main as 41103bf.
close_reason: Delivered executable IME composition epochs and bidirectional editable-surface protocol boundary with real Parley shaping, exact provenance/geometry, multi-selection normalization evidence, atomic failures, retained cancel/commit work, migration docs, and all local gates green.

title: Execute snapshot-range text editing
description:
Add revision-checked, UTF-8-safe selection-set replacement. Each selection is one insertion point and may carry several logically ordered ranges from visual bidi selection; several selections are independent insertion points. Validate the complete set, apply source edits in reverse order, publish once, and return collapsed post-edit selections without pretending revision-bound offsets are durable anchors.

## Required Skills
alder,marten,cedar,badger
design: Document editing remains immutable and transactional. Validate revision, semantic leaf identity, logical range order, UTF-8 boundaries, within-selection source ownership, and overlap across the entire selection set before staging. Delete every range in a selection, insert its replacement once at the first logical boundary, apply selections in reverse source order, and publish once.
acceptance_criteria: Multi-caret insert, replace, backspace, delete, and visual-bidi multi-range replacement work at multibyte boundaries; each selection inserts once; stale revision, invalid UTF-8, unordered range, cross-leaf selection, duplicate insertion point, and overlapping selections fail atomically; exactly the affected paragraph formations are invalidated; returned post-edit selections preserve input order; migration notes and full gates pass.
notes:
Implemented atomic selection-set replacement with one insertion per selection, reverse-source application, document-ordered ChangeSet output, returned new-revision carets, and fail-closed validation. Real Parley proofs cover mixed-bidi multi-range replacement, multi-caret insert, replacement, forward delete, backspace, multibyte/combining clusters, stale/foreign/overlap/UTF-8 failures, and exact two-of-three paragraph invalidation. Evidence: underwood/src/document.rs, underwood/src/selection.rs, underwood_parley/src/lib.rs, docs/proof/selection-transaction-review-2026-07-22.md. Full Definition of Done and portability gates pass.
Remote proof: PR #13 (https://github.com/forest-rs/underwood/pull/13) passed all eight required CI jobs in run 29928462074 and landed on main as 7683ec75. Post-merge push run 29928786846 also completed successfully.
close_reason: Delivered atomic snapshot selection-set replacement with one insertion per independent selection, disjoint visual-bidi range deletion, returned new-revision carets, fail-closed validation, forward-delete/backspace coverage, and exact affected-paragraph retention proof. Full repository, MSRV, no_std, and Wasm gates pass.

title: Map revision-bound scene positions and selection geometry
description:
Map adapter-produced visual clusters and caret affinities through semantic projection into exact revision-bound scene positions. Replace fragment hit testing and point-derived carets, then add bidi-correct selection rectangles and visual/logical movement.

## Required Skills
alder,otter,marten,cedar,badger
design: The paragraph adapter returns portable cluster and caret facts. Underwood validates and maps them to snapshot leaves, stores the scene interaction map, and composes selection geometry without reimplementing text physics.
acceptance_criteria: Exact and closest hits, carets, adapter-owned movements, selection sets, and selection geometry pass ligature, combining, Arabic RTL, mixed bidi, soft and explicit break, whitespace, empty editable text, stale revision, and round-trip conformance. One visual selection can retain multiple logically ordered ranges; several independent selections retain distinct insertion points; geometry tags selection and range ownership. Old approximations are deleted; public migration and full gates pass.
notes:
Exact hit/caret prerequisite landed through PR #12 at 2fa8120. Selection/movement work proceeds on selection-transactions. Parley high-level cursor audit confirms visual movement has affinity-sensitive soft-wrap and bidi transitions; the next slice must consume adapter-owned transition facts rather than merely walking scene caret rectangles or recreating cursor physics in Underwood.
Implemented revision-bound selection sets, visual/logical movement, exact selection geometry, and adapter-owned cursor transitions. One visual bidi selection retains multiple logical ranges while multiple selections remain independent insertion points. Evidence: underwood/src/selection.rs, underwood/src/scene.rs, underwood/src/adapter.rs, underwood_parley/src/lib.rs, docs/proof/selection-transaction-review-2026-07-22.md. Full Definition of Done, MSRV 1.92, x86_64-unknown-none, and wasm32-unknown-unknown gates pass.
Remote proof: exact interaction landed through PR #12 and selection transactions consumed it in merged PR #13; main remains green at 7683ec75 with post-merge run 29928786846 successful.
close_reason: Delivered executable revision-bound multi-selection movement and geometry through adapter-owned Parley cursor facts; mixed-bidi visual selection retains exact disjoint logical ranges and selection/range geometry ownership. Adversarial proof is checked in and full repository, MSRV, no_std, and Wasm gates pass.

title: Interactive semantic document campaign
description:
Turn the live retained-document proof into a directly interactive semantic document with exact Parley-backed cluster hit testing and carets, revision-bound selection, validated range transactions, and a separate IME composition epoch. Deliver coherent landable slices without stabilizing durable anchors or moving host policy into core.

## Required Skills
alder,otter,marten,badger,lynx,rook
design: Parley owns paragraph-local cluster, bidi, affinity, and cursor physics. Underwood owns snapshot positions, semantic mapping, selection geometry, transactions, composition projection, and retained work. The external showcase owns Winit event translation, gestures, focus, blinking, and presentation.
acceptance_criteria: A live user can click and drag through mixed LTR/RTL, ligature, combining, break, whitespace, and empty-text cases; exact carets and selection agree with source; keyboard edits publish one affected-paragraph transaction; IME preedit does not mutate committed text and cancel reuses committed formation; no old fragment/query-point approximation remains; full local and remote gates pass.
notes:
User approved semantic activation as the final interaction slice: exact hit plus SemanticId in Underwood, action or URL association in the showcase, and activation in the host. This is intentionally not a permanent core link schema.
2026-07-22 selection correction: follow the TextKit 2 distinction between the layout manager's array of selections and one selection's logically ordered, possibly noncontiguous ranges. Underwood will expose a revision-bound selection set; each member is one insertion point with anchor, extent, logical/visual interpretation, affinity, and one or more ranges. Mixed-bidi visual selection must not be collapsed to a logical union. Multi-caret editing validates the whole set, inserts once per selection, and publishes atomically.

title: Build the live resizable document showcase
description:
Present one genuine semantic Underwood document in a native resizable window. Resize must drive finite-width retained paragraph formation; imaging and imaging_vello_cpu must rasterize the portable scene; softbuffer only presents pixels. Include honest local-edit, paint-only, reset, and optional variable-axis animation evidence without adding dependencies to production crates.

## Required Skills
alder,otter,badger,wren
design: The external showcase owns winit events, softbuffer presentation, composition, and narration. Underwood owns the document, invalidation, formation, and TextScene; underwood_parley owns shaping and font selection; imaging_vello_cpu owns rasterization.
acceptance_criteria: cargo run -p underwood_showcase opens a resizable window; one heading/deck/body document visibly reflows; edit and paint controls report their real WorkReport effects; animation executes a real variable-font axis; production dependency edges remain unchanged; fmt, clippy -D warnings, tests, xtask policy, and a live smoke run pass.
notes:
Implemented one ten-paragraph semantic document through one retained LayoutEngine and TextScene into imaging, imaging_vello_cpu, and softbuffer. Release host reflows on resize; local edit shapes exactly one paragraph and reuses nine; paint changes only PaintTable brushes with zero analysis/shaping/flow/geometry and all ten reused; animated wght and static wdth instances execute real normalized coordinates; liga on/off yields four/six glyphs; Arabic uses exact Noto Kufi fallback with RTL/source/zero-advance mark evidence; H1 owns a real gradient brush. Reviewer Must and Should findings resolved: stable paint slots, pauseable phase-continuous animation, drift-free deadlines, logical keys, explicit clipping, split prep/render timing, truthful copy, and showcase-local Arabic proof. Added Design-0008, review evidence, proof-ledger entry, README/migration docs, and example-only native dependencies with explicit winit features. Full local stable, MSRV 1.92, rustdoc, no_std targets, repository/text/Beads policy, and live release smoke gates pass. Remote CI and landing remain.
Draft PR #11 opened at https://github.com/forest-rs/underwood/pull/11 from commit 813436f. Eight required CI jobs queued in Actions run 29905895600.
CI run 29905972485 exposed one cross-platform Clippy issue in the native showcase host: Linux and Windows reject an explicit drop of softbuffer::Buffer because those backend buffer types do not implement Drop. Replaced the lifetime workaround with a present_frame helper whose scope naturally ends the surface/buffer borrow. The existing malformed-frame test exercises the error path; focused showcase Clippy/tests and full workspace Clippy/tests pass locally.
close_reason: Complete live retained semantic-document showcase with one real resizable document, mixed LTR/RTL shaping, variable axes, OpenType features, H1 gradient paint, honest retained-work controls, split preparation/render timing, explicit clipping, successful live demonstration, full local validation, and all eight jobs green in GitHub Actions run 29911682384. Follow-up Arabic justification remains tracked as und-oh0.5.2.

title: Upstream bounded break reshaping and retire the Parley fork pin
description:
Move the bounded break-sensitive reshaping candidate from the temporary public waywardmonkeys/parley pin into linebender/parley, then repin every Underwood Parley-family dependency to one immutable upstream revision.

## Required Skills
otter
design: Preserve Parley Core ownership of retained reshape inputs, unsafe-region discovery, and bounded break/concat execution. Underwood keeps only break selection, backtracking, portable lowering, and work evidence. Review with Tom, adjust the candidate at its source, and do not grow a permanent fork or a second shaping implementation.

title: Replace proportional ligature paint coverage with conformance-backed coverage
description:
Remove the character-count proportional coverage approximation used to split paint inside shaped glyphs. Establish a paragraph-preparation seam whose source coverage is correct for supported scripts and fails explicitly where exact coverage is unavailable.

## Required Skills
alder,otter,marten
design: Keep paint identity owned by Underwood and shaping mechanics owned by Parley. Prefer an upstream primitive over growing generic text mechanics in underwood_parley.
acceptance_criteria: Conformance tests cover Latin ligatures, Arabic cursive text, bidi boundaries, and unsupported cases. No proportional character-count approximation is presented as conformant, and any contingency has an explicit removal gate.
notes:
Must-level visual defect confirmed by an independent Arabic audit. Source text, bidi order, and Noto Kufi shaping are correct, but each beh (ب) is shaped as dotless base uni066E plus zero-advance dotbelowar. underwood_parley::paint_coverage derives clip width from abs(glyph.advance), giving the mark a zero-width Rect; scene lowering preserves it and the imaging_vello_cpu adapter clips the dot away. The poster therefore renders malformed dotless letters. Fix the general renderer-neutral contract using real ink coverage (also covers combining marks and overhangs), add a public product-path beh regression plus combining/overhang cases, and update the exact RGBA snapshot. Snapshot reproducibility alone is not visual conformance.
Implementation candidate: waywardmonkeys/parley:ink-coverage-primitives at 6a9d91739e47b02180e10fc9fb54eed3e9405b7a, based on bounded reshape 181664b. Parley Core now exposes callback-scoped, variation-aware glyph ink bounds without a new dependency. Roboto Flex and Roboto Regular contain no GDEF LigCaretList, so the former proportional ffi split has been deleted rather than relabeled. Underwood now uses real outline bounds, preserves zero-advance marks and overhangs, applies synthetic skew to coverage, and returns UnsupportedPaintCoverage for one glyph crossing paint runs or for synthetic embolden whose ink expansion is unavailable. und-oh0.2.8 is the explicit removal gate for supported multi-paint ligatures.
Supersedes the 6a9d917 candidate note: the review commit was amended only to add direct variable-instance ink-bounds evidence. Current exact candidate and Underwood pin: d12c801d8fd298ff095f1ec903b6adaa732fcef2.
Remote proof: forest-rs/underwood PR #10, commit 6ee00df1a0d66dfa2658211aa347eb5a50ee2ed9, CI run 29900013909. All eight jobs passed across Linux, macOS, Windows, MSRV, rustdoc, repository policy, and no_std portability. Poster PNG SHA-256 be7eaf53d4cccd72ae253433ffe7ee82a74c431a70f74c8c13787110499c4a11 was visually inspected with the Arabic dots present.
Superseded by Design-0010 and und-oh0.2.9: outline-derived ink bounds and Parley candidate d12c801 are historical evidence, not the current rendering contract or dependency pin. Ordinary glyph paint is unclipped, actual painted extent belongs to the renderer, and Underwood now pins bounded-reshape-only Parley commit 44d155e.
close_reason: Replaced proportional paint geometry with variation-aware glyph ink bounds; restored visible zero-advance Arabic marks and overhangs; made unsupported split-paint glyphs explicit; added upstream Parley Core candidate d12c801, visual/pixel proof, migration record, and removal gate und-oh0.2.8; all local and PR #10 remote gates pass.

title: ADR-0004: Parley boundary and contingency
description:
Ratify the Parley preparation boundary and upstream contingency.

## Decision

Pending human ratification and upstream investigation. The starting architecture isolates all adaptation in underwood_parley and never leaks a fork through the facade.

## Rationale

Underwood needs retained seams without duplicating text physics or allowing upstream review latency to become an accidental architectural veto.

## Alternatives Considered

- Depend permanently on high-level Layout with paint payloads: couples the wrong cache identities.
- Fork Parley broadly: creates an unsustainable second text engine.
- Wait indefinitely for every upstream seam: surrenders product sequencing to external timing.
design: Specify analysis, itemization, shaping and break inputs and outputs; pinned revision policy; upstream contribution path; review threshold; patch ownership and removal; breaker allowance over parley_core; and divergence conformance.
acceptance_criteria: ADR defines the underwood_parley fence, required prepared contracts, version policy, upstream workflow, evidence threshold, patch lifecycle, conformance matrix, prohibited leakage, and human ratification.
notes: 2026-07-21: Bruce Mitchener explicitly ratified the decision record. Acceptance is scoped exactly as documented and does not implicitly authorize a production dependency, unsafe code, a new public API, or an unreviewed workspace boundary.

title: ADR-0003: text-data provisioning and identity
description:
Ratify explicit international text-data provisioning and identity.

## Decision

Pending human ratification. The starting architecture uses immutable versioned bundles with deliberate minimal and full tiers.

## Rationale

Unicode, segmentation, line breaking, locale, and hyphenation data affect correctness, caches, replay, and WebAssembly budgets and cannot remain ambient.

## Alternatives Considered

- Baked unversioned data: convenient but nondeterministic across upgrades.
- Inner-loop provider dispatch: flexible but costly and unnecessary.
- Full data everywhere: simplest capability story but unacceptable for constrained targets.

Ratify resumable flow and virtual extent semantics.

## Decision

Pending human ratification after long-book and million-line traces. The starting architecture retains versioned checkpoints and honest estimated or measured extents.

## Rationale

Pagination and virtualization require explicit resumable state; eager global flow and undisclosed estimates violate the performance and observability contracts.

## Alternatives Considered

- Eager full flow: simple but globally expensive.
- Opaque implementation checkpoints: cannot support deterministic diagnostics or remote preparation.
- UI-owned scroll correction: duplicates private layout reasoning in hosts.

title: ADR-0001: position and canonical storage contract
description:
Ratify the position and canonical storage contract.

## Decision

Pending human ratification after the required traces. The starting architecture has three semantic position forms behind a concrete Document facade.

## Rationale

Sparse human identity, dense authored ranges, and dense derived ranges have incompatible persistence and cost contracts; collaboration challenges storage authority.

## Alternatives Considered

- Universal stable anchors: durable but prohibitive for dense layers.
- Universal revision offsets: compact but incorrect for persistent references.
- Generic Document storage parameter: flexible but leaks representation into hot paths and public APIs.
design: Decision requires million-span derived and authored traces plus Loro cursor and mark prototypes. Compare canonical-first and CRDT-authoritative publication without exposing a storage-generic Document.
acceptance_criteria: ADR states position semantics and lifecycle, edit mapping laws, storage fence, adapter boundary, measurable budgets, competing prototype plan, migration implications, and human-approved chosen contract or explicit experiment gate.
notes: 2026-07-21: Bruce Mitchener explicitly ratified the decision record. Acceptance is scoped exactly as documented and does not implicitly authorize a production dependency, unsafe code, a new public API, or an unreviewed workspace boundary.
close_reason: Accepted by Bruce Mitchener on 2026-07-21; durable decision and remaining gates are recorded in the corresponding ADR.

title: Charter-000: spearhead, proof, and stewardship
description:
Ratify the product, proof, and stewardship constitution.

## Decision

Pending human ratification. The starting recommendation is a funded commons anchored by the living agent document as flagship.

## Rationale

The architecture needs a wanted product, literal proof law, concentrated active work, and durable conformance ownership before compatibility promises.

## Alternatives Considered

- Infrastructure-only project: risks adoption without a carrying product.
- Closed or correctness-tiered open core: conflicts with the common-infrastructure intent.
- Unfunded volunteer commons: does not credibly staff sustained platform conformance.
design: Start from the funded-commons anchored by flagship product recommendation. Preserve engine, formats, corpora, and ordinary adapters as common infrastructure while naming how permanent conformance work is supported.
acceptance_criteria: A checked-in charter names goals and non-goals, flagship and wind tunnels, proof promotion law, active campaign discipline, public-API rule, common infrastructure boundary, initial stewardship commitments, and explicit human ratification or recorded overturning of the recommendation.
notes: 2026-07-21: Bruce Mitchener explicitly ratified Charter-000. Initial funding and ownership remain deliberately honest: no secured recurring funding is claimed; bootstrap stewardship is named; specialist vacancies block corresponding proof claims.
close_reason: Accepted by Bruce Mitchener on 2026-07-21; the durable charter records the flagship, proof law, common boundary, stewardship, funding posture, and contributor path.

title: Remove outline ink bounds as a rendering precondition
description: Stop treating outline-derived glyph rectangles as mandatory renderer clips or complete painted-ink truth. Ordinary source-complete glyphs render unclipped, including synthetic emboldening and fonts whose visible paint is not represented by an outline. Preserve explicit partial-paint clips as a future conformant multi-paint operation. Keep interaction geometry cluster-based and renderer culling or damage conservative when painted extent is unknown.
design: A single-paint glyph is drawn once and its backend owns actual paint extent. Underwood carries source-to-paint ownership; one whole owner needs no glyph clip. Explicit source-complete clipped segments remain representable for future conformant split paint. Do not expose outline-only bounds as universal ink metadata, do not substitute advance rectangles, and do not reject bitmap, color, or synthesized glyphs merely because portable outline extent is unavailable. Cross-paint glyphs remain UnsupportedPaintCoverage until und-oh0.2.8 earns component geometry.
acceptance_criteria: Ordinary Underwood, showcase, headless, and visual-proof lowering draws glyphs without per-fragment clips; explicit segmented coverage lowers to distinct clipped paint operations; synthetic emboldening and native Chinese system fallback prepare successfully; interaction and selection use semantic cluster geometry; stale outline-ink claims are superseded; full local and remote gates pass.
notes:
Runtime evidence: a macOS IME commit of Chinese text resolved a real Han system font but failed as UnsupportedPaintCoverage because synthetic emboldening could not satisfy the mandatory outline clip. This makes the renderer-boundary cleanup a correctness prerequisite for the editor, not merely an optimization.
Implemented and locally validated under Design-0010. Ordinary glyphs are unclipped; exact explicit split lowering remains; synthetic embolden, native Han IME fallback, Arabic marks, mixed-script insertion, headless, and CPU snapshot pass. Clean Parley bounded-reshape-only commit 44d155e is pushed to waywardmonkeys/bounded-break-reshape and every workspace member uses it. Awaiting remote CI before closure.
Remote Definition of Done passed for renderer-only PR #15 at commit 384fc74 in GitHub Actions run 29970742844. All eight required jobs are green across Linux, macOS, Windows, formatting/text policy, repository policy, Rust 1.92 MSRV, denied-warning rustdoc, x86_64-unknown-none, and wasm32-unknown-unknown.
close_reason: Renderer-owned glyph paint extent is complete: ordinary glyphs render unclipped; explicit partial paint remains validated; synthetic embolden and native Han fallback prepare; all source provenance is retained; the clean Parley bounded-reshape pin is in place; local and remote gates pass.

title: Specify and upstream conformant multi-paint ligature coverage
description:
Define the renderer-neutral rule for a paint boundary inside one shaped glyph, then expose the smallest generally useful Parley Core mechanism needed to execute it. Determine with specification and cross-engine evidence whether GDEF ligature carets are sufficient, whether affected source must be reshaped at the paint boundary, or whether another glyph-component representation is required. Keep UnsupportedPaintCoverage until the answer is proven; never restore character-count geometry.

## Required Skills
alder,otter,marten
design: Parley owns reusable shaping and font-derived component facts. Underwood owns paint identity and maps an accepted Parley result into portable scene fragments. The result must remain useful to callers that do not adopt Parley LayoutData or Underwood's document model.
acceptance_criteria: A written conformance rule covers Latin and RTL ligatures, fonts with and without GDEF caret data, variable instances, and overlapping outlines; a cross-engine or normative oracle justifies the rule; the required small Parley API is reviewable upstream; Underwood removes UnsupportedPaintCoverage only for cases the new evidence actually supports; no source-count or advance-fraction approximation remains.

title: Converge Fontique cluster selection with Parley
description:
Replace or formally minimize underwood_parley's local Fontique Query-to-Parley Core cluster coverage callback. The current glue mirrors high-level Parley's private selector semantics and can drift around partial coverage, emoji injection, and future fallback behavior.

## Required Skills
alder,otter,lynx
design: Fontique continues to own family/attribute/fallback/coverage candidates, and Parley owns the shaping selector seam. Prefer a small reusable upstream primitive or a conformance-locked callback contract; do not move matching algorithms into Underwood or expose engine types through its facade.
acceptance_criteria: Underwood and high-level Parley share one reusable selector primitive or execute a common conformance corpus covering complete, partial, absent, combining-mark, emoji, and script/language fallback clusters; local glue is reduced to data conversion; any upstream dependency change follows ADR-0004; the exact temporary lifecycle and removal gate are recorded.
notes: Discovered during Design-0004 execution. Current underwood_parley::select_font is deliberately limited to Query setup plus CharCluster coverage arbitration required by Parley Core; it rejects zero-coverage candidates rather than emitting accidental .notdef success. Keep aligned with high-level Parley's private FontSelector until a reusable seam/common corpus exists.

title: Converge a canonical rich-document preparation call site

## Required Skills
alder,cedar,otter
design: Keep the renderer adapter external and provisional until a second consumer proves reuse. Concentrate on computed style resolution, stable paint references, request construction, and useful work reporting rather than a convenience facade that hides ownership.
acceptance_criteria: The visual proof and headless example can share one obvious public workflow; a poster-like specimen uses one heterogeneous document rather than per-label documents; paint references are validated at a clear boundary; WorkReport remains observable; rustdoc includes before-and-after migration guidance and a human-approved public API review.
notes:
Canonical callsite checkpoint: the headless example and CPU poster use Document -> complete StyleMap -> SceneRequest -> LayoutEngine. One three-paragraph semantic document now carries mixed sizes, line heights, paint slots, liga controls, and explicit Roboto Flex axes into one TextScene; Design-0003 and crate rustdoc include the migration.
Final validation: the canonical public Document -> StyleMap -> SceneRequest -> LayoutEngine workflow is shared by the headless and visual examples; the poster specimen uses one heterogeneous semantic document; paint slots and WorkReport remain explicit. GitHub Actions run 29849889488 passed all eight jobs.
close_reason: Canonical rich-document preparation callsite, complete-style migration rustdoc, paint validation, observable work reporting, and one-document poster specimen are implemented and remotely green.

## Required Skills

acceptance_criteria: The upstream revision and retained result contract are recorded, local duplicate ownership machinery is removed or explicitly justified, and cache/invalidation tests remain green through public Underwood contracts.

Implementation now retains one reusable parley_core::ShapedText per paragraph at exact Parley 6c81e1d, removes PhysicsRun/PhysicsGlyph copy storage, and lowers directly into the portable PreparedParagraph boundary. Focused conformance proves full ffi source ownership, RTL visual traversal with logical UTF-8 ranges, honest glyphless newline runs, rejection of glyphless ordinary text, and cache recovery after failed partial shaping. Focused tests and Clippy are green; benchmark, adversarial review, and full workspace gates remain.
Adversarial review resolved four Must findings: RTL logical-vs-visual traversal, honest control-only glyphless runs, stale identity after partial-shape failure, and u16-relative cluster offsets for >64 KiB items. Same-machine product benchmark against main 867acb7 remains within -2.2% to +0.9% across all five workloads; evidence is in docs/proof/retained-shaped-text-benchmark-2026-07-22.md and docs/proof/retained-shaped-text-review-2026-07-22.md. Full repository validation and remote CI remain.
Draft PR #8 opened: https://github.com/forest-rs/underwood/pull/8 at c056166. Full local Definition of Done is green; awaiting GitHub Actions before proof promotion and landing.
Remote Definition of Done passed in GitHub Actions run 29864780006: all eight jobs green across Linux, macOS, Windows, MSRV, rustdoc, repository/text policy, bare-metal, and WebAssembly.

title: Specify Arabic justification and kashida/tatweel formation
description:
Separate authored U+0640 preservation from automatic Arabic line justification. Define how a finite-width formation request selects valid kashida opportunities, chooses stretch amounts, reshapes affected Arabic spans, preserves logical source and caret semantics, and reports invalidated work.

## Required Skills
alder,otter,marten
design: Parley owns script-aware justification opportunities, shaping response, and reusable font/text physics. Underwood owns the paragraph/region justification request, line-width policy, retained invalidation, semantic mapping, and portable output. Automatic kashida must not mutate the authored document or manufacture source characters in the renderer.
acceptance_criteria: A written conformance rule distinguishes authored tatweel from generated justification; a named Arabic corpus covers joining groups, marks, mixed bidi, fonts with and without suitable support, narrow/overfull lines, and copy/hit/caret source behavior; a reference or cross-engine oracle justifies opportunity and stretch choices; the smallest reusable Parley seam is identified; Underwood executes at least one automatic-kashida line through public formation and scene APIs with honest work reporting; authored U+0640 remains source-complete.
